questions.question: Why is PHP considered a server-side language?
options: A) Because it commonly runs on the server to generate responses before content reaches the browser
B) Because browsers compile it directly
C) Because it cannot process forms
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because it commonly runs on the server to generate responses before content reaches the browser

questions.question: What is the practical purpose of form handling in PHP?
options: A) Processing user input, validating it, and producing server-side behavior
questions.correctAnswer: A) Processing user input, validating it, and producing server-side behavior

questions.question: Why are sessions useful?
options: A) They help preserve user-related state across multiple requests
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help preserve user-related state across multiple requests

questions.question: What is the benefit of object-oriented programming in PHP?
options: A) It helps organize behavior and responsibilities into reusable application structures
B) It removes the need for functions
C) It only matters in command-line scripts
questions.correctAnswer: A) It helps organize behavior and responsibilities into reusable application structures

questions.question: Why is Composer important in modern PHP development?
options: A) It manages dependencies and autoloading for maintainable projects
B) It is only for databases
questions.correctAnswer: A) It manages dependencies and autoloading for maintainable projects

questions.question: What problem do prepared statements solve?
options: A) They help separate SQL structure from user data and reduce injection risk
C) They only improve HTML rendering
D) They remove the need for indexes
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help separate SQL structure from user data and reduce injection risk

questions.question: Why does validation matter for PHP applications?
options: A) It prevents bad or unsafe input from flowing deeper into the system
B) It only affects frontend layout
D) It is only useful for file uploads
questions.correctAnswer: A) It prevents bad or unsafe input from flowing deeper into the system

questions.question: What is the role of a framework like Laravel or Symfony?
options: A) Providing structure and reusable patterns for routing, services, auth, and persistence
C) Removing the need for HTTP
questions.correctAnswer: A) Providing structure and reusable patterns for routing, services, auth, and persistence

questions.question: Why do PHP applications still need database design knowledge?
options: A) Because frameworks do not remove the need for sound data modeling and query decisions
B) Because PHP cannot connect to databases
D) Because SQL is optional in all apps
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because frameworks do not remove the need for sound data modeling and query decisions

questions.question: What is the purpose of authentication in a PHP app?
options: A) Identifying users before protected features are accessed
questions.correctAnswer: A) Identifying users before protected features are accessed

questions.question: Why is authorization different from authentication?
options: A) Authentication identifies the user, authorization checks allowed actions
B) Authorization happens before login and authentication after logout
questions.correctAnswer: A) Authentication identifies the user, authorization checks allowed actions

questions.question: What does CSRF protection address?
options: A) Unwanted cross-site requests executed using a user's existing session context
questions.correctAnswer: A) Unwanted cross-site requests executed using a user's existing session context

questions.question: Why are automated tests useful in PHP codebases?
options: A) They help verify application behavior and reduce regressions as features change
C) They only matter for package authors
questions.correctAnswer: A) They help verify application behavior and reduce regressions as features change

questions.question: What is the practical value of queues or background jobs?
options: A) Moving slow or retryable work out of the main request cycle
questions.correctAnswer: A) Moving slow or retryable work out of the main request cycle

questions.question: Why does caching matter in PHP applications?
options: A) It can reduce repeated expensive work and improve response performance
C) It only matters in local development
questions.correctAnswer: A) It can reduce repeated expensive work and improve response performance

questions.question: What is the role of environment-specific configuration?
options: A) Allowing the same app to run safely with different settings across environments
questions.correctAnswer: A) Allowing the same app to run safely with different settings across environments

questions.question: Why is deployment knowledge relevant to PHP developers?
options: A) Because runtime setup, web server behavior, configuration, and hosting still affect production success
B) Because deployment is always handled by designers
C) Because PHP apps do not need servers
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because runtime setup, web server behavior, configuration, and hosting still affect production success

questions.question: What is the purpose of logging and monitoring in production?
options: A) Observing failures, performance problems, and real application behavior
questions.correctAnswer: A) Observing failures, performance problems, and real application behavior

questions.question: Why is secure coding especially important in server-rendered or API-backed PHP apps?
options: A) Because they process user input, credentials, and data access on the server
B) Because PHP cannot validate input
C) Because only JavaScript apps face attacks
D) Because frameworks prevent all security issues
questions.correctAnswer: A) Because they process user input, credentials, and data access on the server

questions.question: What is the strongest way to learn PHP effectively today?
options: A) Build modern applications that combine forms, auth, persistence, testing, and deployment
C) Avoid frameworks and tooling forever
questions.correctAnswer: A) Build modern applications that combine forms, auth, persistence, testing, and deployment

questions.question: Which PHP function outputs text?
questions.correctAnswer: B) echo()

questions.question: Which PHP symbol starts variable?
